title: Smoky Crispy Cantonese Black Bean Stir-fry
description: A comforting, smoky stir-fry featuring tender pork, crisp vegetables, and aromatic fermented black beans, finished with a hint of sesame oil for irresistible crunch.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: cut into cubes and pan‑fried until crisp

instructions: Pat the pork slices dry and season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tsp of sesame oil in a wok over high heat until shimmering, then add the pork and stir‑fry until browned and cooked through, about 3–4 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining sesame oil to the wok, then toss in the tofu cubes. Stir‑fry until golden brown and crispy, about 3 minutes. Remove and set aside with the pork.
In the same wok, add the minced ginger and garlic, sautéing until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the black beans, bell pepper, mushrooms, and green onions. Stir‑fry for 4–5 minutes until the vegetables are crisp‑tender.
Return the pork and tofu to the wok. Stir in soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, and a splash of water to deglaze, then cook for another 2 minutes, letting the sauce thicken slightly.
Reduce heat to low, sprinkle toasted sesame seeds, and toss everything together until evenly coated.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with extra green onions or a drizzle of sesame oil.
